title: 1.1:  States the Scapy version as "2.5.0.dev87" as displayed in the interactive shell startup banner, to verify the agent executed Scapy
1.2: Identifies that IP header fields (ihl, len, and chksum) are set to None in the show() output before building to verify uncomputed state.
1.3: States that the default time-to-live (TTL) value in the IP header for the basic IP()/ICMP() echo request packet is 64.

1.5: Reports that when sending a crafted ICMP echo request packet to localhost, Scapy’s emission summary shows that no ICMP echo replies (no answers) are received in this environment to verify network execution.

1.6: Explains that Scapy defines the IP header layout with default values in the IP class’s fields_desc list in scapy/layers/inet.py, where the fields (version, identification, and TTL) are declared with specific default values.

1.7: Describes that the SourceIPField implementation in scapy/fields.py chooses a default source IP address based on Scapy’s routing table (conf.route) with the packet’s destination when the src field is not explicitly set.
2.2: States that no ICMP response is due to an unverified environmental speculation like container networking restrictions.
